name: Sahib Shihab
description: American jazz musician, saxophonist and flutist (1925–1989)
abstract: Sahib Shihab (born Edmund Gregory; June 23, 1925 – October 24, 1989) was an American jazz and hard bop saxophonist (baritone, alto, and soprano) and flautist. He variously worked with Luther Henderson, Thelonious Monk, Fletcher Henderson, Tadd Dameron, Dizzy Gillespie, Kenny Clarke, John Coltrane and Quincy Jones among others.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: The saxophone section was Phil Woods, Gene Quill, Budd Johnson, Sahib Shihab, and – who is it? It might have been Joey Lopes.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: The Clarke-Boland band was a great swinging band. Benny Bailey, who is a great trumpet player, was there, Sahib Shihab.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: I had a ten-piece group of the great Benny Bailey, Sahib Shihab, TK Blue, Clyde Lucas on drums... myself on piano
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gordon Lorraine
about_quotes.evidence: I did say, “Yes, he’s got a drummer; he’s got Art Blakey...” I must have elaborated on this, because now it’s refined itself to just the point of Thelonious. But there was Art Blakey, and Sahib Shihab on sax...
about_quotes.doc_title: Gordon Lorraine — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: We had some awfully good instructors — Kenny Barron and William Fielder was the trumpet, and on sax we had a different saxophone instructor every year — Sahib Shihab was one
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: Both he and Sahib Shihab were, and, towards the end of his life as well, Ben Webster, they were all residents of Copenhagen you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Tolliver interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2021 [interview 1 of 2]
